seoTitle: Lucky Bay vs Lyndhurst — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lucky Bay and Lyndhurst. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lucky Bay vs Lyndhurst: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lucky Bay and Lyndhurst.
heading: Lucky Bay vs Lyndhurst — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lucky Bay, South Australia and Lyndhurst, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lyndhurst has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Lucky Bay (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lucky Bay or Lyndhurst safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lucky Bay has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Lyndhurst scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lucky Bay scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Lyndhurst scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lucky Bay or Lyndhurst?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lyndhurst has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
